name: usbethernet
description: USB-C Ethernet Adapters for Wired Streaming

embed.title: USB-C Ethernet Adapters
embed.description:
A USB-C → Ethernet adapter plugged into your headset gives it a wired connection to your network. This is the most stable, lowest-latency option for PCVR streaming (Virtual Desktop / Steam Link) because it removes WiFi interference entirely.

**Key rule:** Adapters with a **Realtek** chipset work; **ASIX** chipsets do **not** (they drop the connection when streaming starts). Get the version **with Power Delivery (PD) charging** so the headset charges while you play.

Select an option below for details.

embeds.title: USB-C Ethernet Adapters - Overview
embeds.description:
Use a Realtek-based USB-C Ethernet adapter (with PD charging) to put your headset on a wired LAN with your PC.

**Highly Recommended**
BENFEI USB-C Gigabit (PD) - most-vouched, ~$15-20
MyGatti Gold (100W PD) - cleanest single-cable setup

**Recommended**
Cable Matters 2.5GbE (100W PD) - the canonical pick
Cable Matters 140W charging / generic 2.5GbE w/ PD

**Budget**
Benfei/Ruxely ~$10 (60W PD, 1GbE)

Select a product from the dropdown for details and links.

fields.name: Chipset rule
fields.value:
__Works__: Realtek RTL8153 (1GbE), RTL8156/8156BG (2.5GbE)
__Does NOT work__: ASIX (drops connection when VD opens), RTL8157/8159 (5/10GbE not supported)

fields.name: Cable length
fields.value: USB3 is rated to ~3m. Run the long distance as **Ethernet**, keep the USB-C short, or use a fiber link cable. Avoid slim/flat USB-C cables over 2m.

embeds.title: Cable Matters USB-C to 2.5GbE Adapter (100W PD)
embeds.description: The canonical wired-Ethernet pick. Realtek 2.5GbE confirmed working on the headset, with 100W passthrough charging.

fields.name: Info

__Charging__: 100W PD
__Verdict__: Recommended

embeds.title: Other Working Adapters
embeds.description: These are confirmed working by community members but with fewer reports than the top picks.

fields.name: Cable Matters USB-C to Ethernet (140W Charging)
fields.value: Recommended. Flawless on Q3, keeps the headset charged (~$24). [Amazon Affiliate (USA)](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B0F8TDL49F)

fields.name: Cable Matters / generic 2.5GbE (PD)
fields.value: Recommended. Works great at 2.5GbE - you may need to force 2.5G in Windows + router. The 5GbE variant is not supported. [Amazon Affiliate (USA)](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B0FC61NLVD)

fields.name: Thzzhnno USB-C Ethernet
fields.value: Works - perfect 1Gbps on Q3, but cannot charge at the same time. [Amazon Affiliate (USA)](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B0DDKPSNBQ)

fields.name: BENFEI Secure-Clamp (bring your own cable)
fields.value: Recommended. Cheapest option that lets you use your own USB-C cable; charges via hub. [Amazon Affiliate (USA)](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B0F9WYWT47)

fields.name: UGREEN CM648 / CM848 / CM275
fields.value: Confirmed working (Realtek). UGREEN models 25052 / 35921 / 70446.

fields.name: AXAGON ADE-TXPD
fields.value: Confirmed working on Q3.

fields.name: Hoco UA37 (RJ45 to Type-C)
fields.value: Probably OK - limited reports.

fields.name: Fazn Type-C to 1000Mbps (PD)
fields.value: Works (used daily for sim racing). Regional - Bulgaria/Greece (plesio.bg).

pages.name: cables
pages.title: USB-C & Fiber Cables
pages.description: Cables to pair with your adapter

embeds.title: USB-C & Fiber Cables
embeds.description: Run the long distance as **Ethernet** and keep the USB-C between adapter and headset short, or use a fiber link cable for long runs. USB3 is rated to ~3m; 5m+ copper breaks spec. Avoid slim/flat cables over 2m.

fields.name: Kuject 16ft USB-C Link Cable
fields.value: Highly recommended - the most-recommended link cable, especially paired with the MyGatti adapter. (See also the /linkcables command.)

fields.name: Meta / Oculus Official Link Cable (fiber)
fields.value: Works great - high-quality fiber optic, but expensive.

fields.name: Cheaper fiber options
fields.value: Insignia fiber (~$15 on eBay, built like the Meta cable), plus 'onn' (Walmart) and CableCreations fiber.

fields.name: Confirmed-working USB-C cables
fields.value: All tested full-speed: [B09FDWG61C](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B09FDWG61C) / [B09VKY632R](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B09VKY632R) / [B0BRCMGJS3](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B0BRCMGJS3) (the black one is thinner/more flexible).

fields.name: USB-C cable (worked without flipping)
fields.value: Worked right away with several adapters. [Amazon Affiliate (USA)](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B0DLNW1T8S)

fields.name: Kiwidesign L1 Plus charging link cable
fields.value: Works, great quality.

embeds.title: Couplers, Chargers & Switches
embeds.description: Optional extras to clean up or extend your wired setup.

fields.name: USB-C female-to-female coupler
fields.value: Keeps the dongle off the headset - pair with a long USB-C extension. [Amazon Affiliate (USA)](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B0BRCQ2G1B)

fields.name: 65W USB-C power brick
fields.value: Meta says 18W minimum / 45W for fast charge; 45W is the common sweet spot. [Amazon Affiliate (USA)](https://vrdiscord.com/go/B087MD5MYH)

fields.name: Cheap 2.5Gb switch
fields.value: ~$30 4-port 2.5GbE switch - more stable than Internet Connection Sharing. TP-Link / NETGEAR gigabit switches also work.

embeds.title: Connect the Adapter Directly to Your PC (ICS)
embeds.description: You can plug the adapter straight into your PC (no router needed) using Windows Internet Connection Sharing. Enable ICS and follow the persistence step (set the service to automatic + the registry key) so it survives reboots. Alternatively run a standalone DHCP server, or use a cheap 2.5Gb switch for more stability.

fields.name: Guide
fields.value: [Crayphish vrdocs (ICS setup, Step 5)](https://github.com/Crayphish/vrdocs)
